name: Spicy Bean Goulash
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Red kidney beans, soaked overnight
1 tablespoon Miso (soy paste), dissolved in a little water
recipeInstructions:
This simple goulash recipe is from "Vegetarian Cooking," an RD Home Handbook Publication.
1. Drain the beans, cover with plenty of fresh water, and bring to a boil uncovered. Boil fast for 10 minutes.
2. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 40 minutes or until soft. Drain, reserving liquid.
3. Heat the oil in a large saucepan and gently fry the onion in the oil for 4 to 5 minutes or until soft.
4. Add the garlic, chile, peppers, and mushrooms and cook for five minutes. Cover and cook the mixture over a very gentle heat for 15-20 minutes.
5. Add the cooked beans, potato, paprika, thyme, tomato paste, and miso to the stew.
6. Simmer gently for 30 minutes, adding a little bean liquid if necessary. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Serve hot with rice or noodles. Serves four.
